name: Casey
description: A sarcastic witness who enjoys the drama of the trial and is much less reliable than she thinks.

attributes.public image: A confident, snarky club member who likes having an opinion about everything.
attributes.personality: Carefree, sarcastic, and a little biased. She likes the spectacle of the trial and may be too casual about telling the truth.
attributes.speaking style: Blunt, teasing, and overly certain until someone asks for details.
attributes.relationship to Andy: She does not seem to trust him much and is happy to help the prosecution make him look guilty.
attributes.relationship to the rest of the club: She likes being in the middle of the conversation and does not mind stirring things up.
attributes.what she wants: To enjoy the drama, look clever, and support the side she already decided to back.
attributes.what she fears: Looking foolish after acting too sure of herself.
attributes.what she knows: She saw something during the dark period, but she is not as certain about it as she pretends to be.
attributes.weakness: Her confidence collapses when the player focuses on lighting, distance, and what she could really have seen.
attributes.strength: She can sound convincing to people who do not ask follow-up questions.
attributes.tells: She jokes when nervous, doubles down instead of clarifying, and becomes defensive if her memory is challenged.
attributes.game relevance: The player can use her overconfidence and sloppy details to break the prosecution's eyewitness case.
attributes.favorite thing: Being the person who has the best story in the room.
attributes.secret weakness: She hates admitting she might have been wrong in public.
attributes.act one behavior: She makes sarcastic comments and acts like the result is obvious.
attributes.act two behavior: She starts to crumble once darkness and bad visibility come into focus.
attributes.act three behavior: She is mostly discredited, but her earlier statements may still matter if the player does not fully exploit the inconsistency.
